content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need the same single file, so a clean file-level split isn't possible - let's split by function/region instead. My plan: add a module-level registry dict (e.g. _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}) plus new functions register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name), placed as new top-level functions (I'll put them near the bottom of the file, after parse_pydantic_schema, to avoid touching your area). The only overlap risk: I need one line inside _template_from_str and _template_from_file to merge the global registry into env.filters (env.filters.update(_FILTERS)), with local filters taking precedence over global ones. Can you share the exact signature you're adding for local filters (e.g. does prompt()/from_str()/from_file() gain a filters: Dict param?) and where you call env.filters.update(local_filters) in those two classmethods? I'll place my update(_FILTERS) call immediately BEFORE your local-filters update line so local always wins, and otherwise touch nothing else in those functions.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented global filter registry: added module-level _FILTERS dict near the top imports, register_filter()/unregister_filter() appended after parse_pydantic_schema at the bottom, and a single env.filters.update(_FILTERS) call inserted in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (placed right after the built-in filters are set / env is created, before returning) - matching the agreed insertion point relative to your local-filters update so local filters still win. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass, plus manual smoke test confirming register/unregister work and that already-compiled templates keep their filters (registry is read at compile time, same semantics as your local filters param). Diff written to patch.txt. Thanks for the clean handoff.
